title: Sensor system
abstract: Described are systems and methods to provide agnostic sensor data obtained from a sensor and transmitted to a central database by a transmitter device (102). In one aspect, a value sensed by a sensor component is converted at the transmitter device (102) to an agnostic value defined by a dimensionless universal scale and offset. The agnostic value is then sent to a data acquisition database (750) where it is converted back to the original value. This is accomplished by first providing the data acquisition database (750) with a sensor definition of a scale and offset used to convert the sensor values in addition to any sensor indicia and other parameters to display the data acquired at the data acquisition database (750). The wireless sensors database and user interface are also automatically updated when a new sensor type is attached to the network utilizing configuration data that resides in the new sensor that is sent to the database on its first connection.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A sensor component device comprising: a sensor; a microprocessor; a component non-transitory memory; and a component definition retained in the non-transitory memory, the component definition includes component identification and a transformation algorithm that converts any value sensed by the sensor to within a range of universal numerical values, the sensor component device configured to communicatively connect with a master communications device. 2. The sensor component device of claim 1 further comprising at least one sensor adjustable option, the sensor adjustable option corresponding to a subset of predefined master sensor adjustable options. 3. The component device of claim 2 wherein the predefined master sensor adjustable options are retained in a data acquisition database. 4. The sensor component device of claim 3 wherein the data acquisition database is in a cloud server. 5. The sensor component device of claim 1 wherein the sensor is configured to sense a sensor value and transmit the sensor value to the master communications device. 6. The sensor component device of claim 5 wherein the master communications device comprises a conversion algorithm that essentially convert the sensor value to a universal numerical value within the range, the universal numerical value is only useable at a data acquisition database. 7. The sensor component device of claim 6 wherein the data acquisition possesses a reverse conversion algorithm that converts the universal numerical value essentially back to the sensed value. 8. The sensor component device of claim 1 wherein the component definition further includes component manufacturing information, component capabilities, component communication parameters, name and/or part number of the component, configurable component parameters, and reading values provided by the sensor. 9. A computing device comprising: a microcontroller and a non-transitory memory; a plurality of sensor devices; a computing device definition comprising a plurality of sensor device definitions each from a corresponding sensor device of the plurality of sensor devices, each of the sensor device definitions including a plurality of parameters describing the corresponding sensor device and a sensor agnostic value conversion algorithm that is executable by the microprocessor to convert any corresponding sensor value obtained by the corresponding sensor device to a dimensionless agnostic value consisting of one of a predefined range of numerical values; and 

 a computing device data packet that includes the computer device definition and indicia from the computing device and the plurality of sensor devices. 10. The computing device of claim 9 wherein the computing device further comprises a wireless transceiver that communicatively connects to a master database hosted in one or more servers. 11. The computing device of claim 10 wherein the computing device data packet is configured to be transmitted to the master database where the master database is configured to construct a data acquisition file uniquely for the master communications device arrangement. 12. The master communications device arrangement of claim 9 further comprising a hash algorithm that generates a hash of the arrangement definition, the hash algorithm contained in the non-transitory memory. 13. The master communications device arrangement of claim 12 wherein the arrangement definition hash is configured to be sent to a master database and compared against a plurality of hash entries maintained in a hash library in the master database when setting up an arrangement database specifically for the computing device. 14. A method for acquiring sensor information, the method comprising: providing a master database; providing a component possessing at least one sensor and component non-transient memory containing a component definition that includes a transformation 

 algorithm configured to convert any value sensed by the at least one sensor to within a range of universal numerical values; communicatively linking the component to a master communications device, the master communications device comprising a microprocessor and device non transient memory, the device non-transitory memory possessing a device definition; transferring the component definition to the device non-transitory memory; communicatively linking the master communications device with the master database; constructing a data acquisition receptacle for the master communications device and the component; sensing a sensor value at the sensor; transferring the sensor value to the master communications device; at the master communications device, transforming the sensor value to an agnostic value within the range of universal numerical values via the transformation algorithm; transmitting the agnostic value to the master database; at the master database, recovering the sensor value by applying the transformation algorithm in reverse on the agnostic value; and storing the recovered agnostic value in the data acquisition receptacle. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the data receptacle is a table with a different row corresponding to each recovered agnostic value. 16. The method of claim 14 wherein the component definition further comprises a unique ID, name and part number of the component, component parameters that are configurable, and component reading values. 17. The method of claim 16 wherein the component reading values further comprise value display name, value unit, value icon, value display format, value calibration method, and value transformation parameters. 18. The method of claim 17 further comprising before the constructing step and after the communicatively linking the master communications device with the master database step, transmitting the component definition and the device definition to the master database. 19. The method of claim 14 wherein the component definition further includes a configurable sensor parameter that can be set with an upper limit value and/or a lower limit value and further comprising triggering an alarm when one of the sensor readings is at or higher than the upper limit and/or is at or lower than the lower limit. 20. The method of claim 14 wherein the step for communicatively linking the master communications device with the master database is accomplished by wirelessly connecting the master communications device with an Internet access hub that is Internet connected, the master database connected to the Internet. 21. A sensor system comprising: 

 a master communications arrangement that includes a master communications device and at least one component connected to the master communications device, the external component including a sensor that generates sensor data; a master database linked to the master communications arrangement, the link consisting of a wireless communication link; an arrangement definition retained in the master communications arrangement, the arrangement definition comprising a unique indicia and a set of instructions including display instructions of how the reconstructed sensor data is displayed to an end user; a hash comparison engine, in the master database, that compares a hash function of the arrangement definition to a library of hash definitions which if the hash function is not in the library of hash definitions, automatically uploading the arrangement definition to the master database; and only after the master database is in possession of the arrangement definition, a data acquisition table is in the master database, the data acquisition table is generated from the set of instructions applied to the data acquisition table, data acquisition table uniquely tied to the unique indicia. 22. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition includes layout instructions of how the reconstructed sensor data is displayed in the data acquisition table. 23. The sensor system of claim 22 wherein the layout instructions include units of measure, digits after the decimal point, data rounding, scientific notation, icons, and accuracy text. 24. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition further includes data interaction instructions of how the reconstructed sensor data interacts with at least one other input data received from a second external component that is either connected to the master communications device or to a separate master communications device. 25. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition further includes informational text and informational graphics. 26. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition further includes user input prompts for the configuration options. 27. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the master communications arrangement further comprises an action producing component or subcomponent that is responsive to output commands from the master database. 28. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition further comprises an amendment added to the arrangement definition after the arrangement definition is retained in the master database. 29. The sensor system of claim 28 wherein the amendment is added to either the master communications device or the master database. 30. The sensor system of claim 29 wherein the amendment is added when a new component is connected to the master communications arrangement. 31. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the configurations options include sensor input fields configured to receive sensor specific parameters that are manipulated to calibrate the sensor. 32. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the configurations options include frequency of sensor transmissions, frequency of sensor sampling, and frequency of sensor data logging. 33. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the data acquisition table displays an out-of- range message for any agnostic sensor data that does not fall within a preset transformed quantity range. 34. The sensor system of claim 33 wherein the out-of-range message replaces any out of range sensor value. 35. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the arrangement definition further includes data interaction instructions of how the reconstructed a hybrid data set that includes sensor data interacting with at least one other input data received from a second external component that is either connected to the master communications device or to a separate master communications device. 36. The sensor system of claim 21 further comprising a second master communication arrangement that is linked to the master communications arrangement via a communication pathway that does not include the master database. 37. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the set of instructions further including a conversion algorithm that converts the sensor data into agnostic sensor data and converts the agnostic sensor data into reconstructed sensor data. 38. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the set of instructions further including data acquisition instructions of how the reconstructed sensor data is stored to the master database. 39. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the set of instructions further including output instructions that cause an output action based on the reconstructed sensor data. 40. The sensor system of claim 21 wherein the set of instructions further including configuration options that permits the end user to reconfigure at least one parameter corresponding to the sensor data.
